Details: One paper investigated the removal of 4-nonylphenol from water by ozone combined with hydrogen peroxide. The combination of ozone and peroxide can achieve 55 to 90 percent 4-nonylphenol removal, with higher removal associated with longer ozone contact times.  Varying the ozone to peroxide ratio appeared to have no impact on the 4-nonylphenol removal. This technology does not appear to be more effective than removal by ozone alone.
